title: Variables & Constants
description: Use var and let with Swift type inference.

concept.definition: Swift uses var for mutable values and let for immutable constants.
concept.analogy: var is editable, let is locked after assignment.

defaultCode:
let app = "StepViz"
var count = 1
count += 1
print(app, count)

title: Core Data Types
description: Work with Int, Double, String, Bool and basic literals.

concept.definition: Swift has strong static types and clear primitive value models.
concept.analogy: Types are strict containers for specific value kinds.

defaultCode:
let age: Int = 20
let score: Double = 9.5
let name: String = "Ari"
let active: Bool = true
print(age, score, name, active)

title: Functions
description: Define typed functions with parameters and return values.

concept.definition: Swift functions are strongly typed and support argument labels.
concept.analogy: A function is a typed machine that transforms input to output.

title: Control Flow
description: Branch logic using if/else and switch.

concept.definition: Swift control flow selects execution paths with strict condition handling.
concept.analogy: Conditions are route selectors in your program.
keyPoints: switch is exhaustive in many cases

title: Loops
description: Repeat work with for-in and while loops.

concept.definition: Swift loops iterate ranges and collections with clear syntax.
concept.analogy: Loops are repeat tracks for logic blocks.

title: Optionals
description: Represent missing values safely using optionals and unwrapping.

concept.definition: Optionals wrap values that may be absent (nil).
concept.analogy: An optional is a box that may or may not contain a value.

defaultCode:
var name: String? = nil
let safe = name ?? "Guest"
print(safe)

title: Collections
description: Use Array, Dictionary, and Set for grouped data.

concept.definition: Swift collection types are generic and type-safe.
concept.analogy: Collections are typed containers for many values.

title: Structs & Classes
description: Model data with value and reference types.

concept.definition: Structs are value types; classes are reference types.
concept.analogy: Struct copies are snapshots, class copies are shared links.

title: Protocols & Extensions
description: Define contracts and add behavior with extensions.

concept.definition: Protocols define required behavior; extensions add implementations and helpers.
concept.analogy: Protocols are agreements, extensions are capability add-ons.

title: Enums
description: Model finite states and associated values.

concept.definition: Swift enums can represent finite cases and hold associated data.
concept.analogy: Enums are safe status sets with optional payloads.

title: Error Handling
description: Throw and catch typed errors with do/try/catch.

concept.definition: Swift error handling uses throw and do-catch blocks for recoverable failures.
concept.analogy: Errors are controlled exits from risky operations.

title: Async/Await
description: Handle asynchronous tasks with modern Swift concurrency.

concept.definition: Swift concurrency provides async functions and await for structured asynchronous code.
concept.analogy: await pauses one task while keeping app responsive.
